preparationInstructions: Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds. Let them splutter.
Add curry leaves, green chilies, and chopped onions. Sauté until onions turn translucent.
Add turmeric powder and mix well.
Add the boiled and mashed potatoes. Mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes. Season with salt.
Heat a griddle and spread a thin layer of dosa batter in a circular motion.
Drizzle oil around the edges and let it cook until the bottom turns golden brown.
Place the potato masala filling in the center of the dosa.
Fold the dosa from both sides and serve hot with coconut chutney and sambar.

ingredients: Whole spices (bay leaves, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves)
preparationInstructions: Marinate chicken with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, biryani masala, salt, and half of the mint-coriander leaves. Let it rest for 30 minutes.
Wash and soak basmati rice for 30 minutes. Boil rice until 70% cooked. Drain and set aside.
Heat ghee and oil in a heavy-bottomed pan. Fry sliced onions until golden brown. Remove half for garnishing.
Add whole spices to the remaining onions and sauté for a minute.
Add marinated chicken and cook on high heat for 5 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until chicken is 80% done.
Layer the partially cooked rice over the chicken. Sprinkle saffron milk, fried onions, and remaining herbs.
Cover tightly with foil and lid. Cook on low heat (dum) for 25-30 minutes.
Turn off heat and let it rest for 10 minutes before opening.
Gently mix and serve hot with raita and pickle.

ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
preparationInstructions: Preheat oven to 230°C (450°F). If using a pizza stone, place it in the oven while preheating.
Roll out pizza dough into a 12-inch circle on a lightly floured surface.
Mix tomato sauce with minced garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Spread the tomato sauce evenly over the pizza base, leaving a 1-inch border for the crust.
Sprinkle shredded mozzarella cheese evenly over the sauce.
Drizzle olive oil over the top.
Transfer pizza to a baking sheet or pizza peel if using a stone.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until the crust is golden and cheese is bubbling and slightly browned.
Remove from oven and immediately top with fresh basil leaves.
Sprinkle with dried oregano, slice, and serve hot.
Heat butter in a pan and sauté chopped onions until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for a minute until the raw smell disappears.
Add tomato puree and cook for 8-10 minutes until oil separates from the mixture.
Add red chili powder, turmeric powder, and salt. Mix well.
Add half a cup of water and bring to a boil.
Lower the heat and add fresh cream. Stir continuously.
Add paneer cubes and mix gently to coat them with the gravy.
Crush kasuri methi between your palms and add to the curry.
Add garam masala and cook for another 2-3 minutes.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot with naan or rice.

ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (carrots, cabbage, bell peppers, beans)
preparationInstructions: Boil water in a large pot. Add noodles and cook according to package instructions until al dente.
Drain noodles and rinse with cold water. Toss with 1 tbsp oil to prevent sticking. Set aside.
Heat oil in a wok or large pan over high heat.
Add minced garlic and ginger. Stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add all the vegetables and stir-fry on high heat for 2-3 minutes until they are crisp-tender.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, chili sauce, and black pepper. Mix well.
Add the cooked noodles to the wok. Toss everything together for 2-3 minutes.
Add chopped spring onions and give it a final toss.
Adjust seasoning with salt if needed.
Serve hot as a main dish or side.
Place the coffee powder in the upper compartment of a South Indian coffee filter.
Press down gently with the presser.
Pour hot (not boiling) water slowly over the coffee powder.
Cover with the lid and let it brew for 10-15 minutes. The decoction will collect in the lower container.
Heat milk in a saucepan until it comes to a boil.
In a traditional tumbler, add 2-3 tbsp of coffee decoction (adjust to taste).
Add sugar to taste.
Pour hot milk over the decoction.
Pour the coffee back and forth between the tumbler and davara (small bowl) from a height to create froth.
Serve hot in the traditional steel tumbler and davara set.

ingredients: 2 cups mixed vegetables (cabbage, carrots, beans), finely chopped
preparationInstructions: Mix finely chopped vegetables with flour, 1 tbsp cornstarch, salt, and pepper. Add a little water to form a thick mixture.
Heat oil in a deep pan for frying.
Shape the vegetable mixture into small balls and deep fry until golden brown. Drain on paper towels.
In another pan, heat 2 tbsp oil. Add minced garlic, ginger, and green chilies. Sauté for a minute.
Add chopped onions and bell peppers. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add soy sauce, tomato ketchup, and chili sauce. Mix well.
Mix 1 tbsp cornstarch with 1/2 cup water and add to the pan to thicken the sauce.
Add the fried vegetable balls and toss gently to coat with the sauce.
Cook for 2-3 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats the manchurian balls.
Garnish with chopped spring onions and serve hot as an appetizer or with fried rice.
Wash and soak idli rice and fenugreek seeds together for 4-6 hours.
Wash and soak urad dal separately for 4-6 hours.
Grind urad dal with minimal water until you get a fluffy, light batter. Transfer to a large bowl.
Grind the rice with fenugreek seeds to a slightly coarse batter. Add to the urad dal batter.
Mix both batters well. Add salt and mix again.
Cover the bowl and let the batter ferment for 8-12 hours in a warm place until it doubles in volume.
After fermentation, gently mix the batter without deflating it too much.
Grease idli moulds with oil.
Pour batter into each mould, filling them 3/4th full.
Steam the idlis in an idli steamer for 10-12 minutes.
Let them cool for a minute, then remove with a wet spoon.
Serve hot with coconut chutney and sambar.
Prepare sugar syrup: Boil sugar and water with cardamom and saffron until you get a one-string consistency. Add rose water and keep warm.
In a bowl, mix milk powder, flour, and baking soda.
Add ghee and mix until it resembles breadcrumbs.
Gradually add milk and knead into a soft, smooth dough. Do not over-knead.
Cover and let it rest for 10 minutes.
Divide the dough into small portions and roll into smooth, crack-free balls.
Heat oil in a pan on low-medium heat. The oil should not be too hot.
Gently slide the balls into the oil and fry, stirring continuously for even browning.
Fry until they turn dark golden brown. This should take 8-10 minutes on low heat.
Remove from oil and immediately transfer to the warm sugar syrup.
Let them soak for at least 2 hours before serving.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
In a large bowl, mix flour, sugar, salt, baking powder, and baking soda.
Add yogurt and oil. Mix well.
Gradually add warm water and knead into a soft, smooth dough.
Cover with a damp cloth and let it rest for 2-3 hours.
After resting, divide the dough into equal portions.
Roll each portion into an oval or tear-drop shape, about 1/4 inch thick.
Brush one side with water and sprinkle nigella seeds.
Heat a tawa or griddle over medium-high heat.
Place the naan on the tawa with the wet side down. Cook until bubbles appear.
Flip and cook the other side. You can also finish it directly over the flame for charred spots.
Brush generously with butter while hot.
Serve warm with your favorite curry.
